name: gpu_vram_copy
title: GPU memory copy bandwidth
short_description: Bandwidth for copying data within the graphics processor's memory.

structured_text.description: Copies large buffers from one region of the graphics processor's local memory to another, reading and writing at the same time over a working set far larger than any on chip cache. This combined pattern matches how most GPU kernels behave, reading data, transforming it and writing it back.
structured_text.metric_explanation: Bandwidth is the number of bytes moved per second counting both the read and the write side, reported in GB/s.
structured_text.value_interpretation: Higher is better. Copy bandwidth reflects real GPU data movement more closely than read or write alone, since both happen together.
structured_text.reasoning: Most GPU kernels both read and write memory, so a copy test that does both at once is the most representative single measure of local memory throughput.

domain_gaming.explanation: Moving and transforming texture and buffer data combines reads and writes each frame.

domain_office.explanation: Office work moves little data through GPU memory.

domain_software_development.explanation: Mostly relevant when developing GPU accelerated software.

domain_graphics_design.explanation: Filtering images reads layers, transforms them and writes them back in GPU memory.

domain_video_editing.explanation: Processing frames reads and writes high resolution buffers together in GPU memory.

domain_3d_rendering.explanation: Moving scene and buffer data combines reads and writes during GPU rendering.

domain_audio_production.explanation: Audio work moves little data through GPU memory.

domain_cad_engineering.explanation: Viewport and solver kernels stream data in and out of GPU memory together.

domain_scientific_computing.explanation: Stencil and update kernels read and write large arrays together in GPU memory.

domain_ai_training.explanation: Reading weights and writing activations together is copy heavy GPU work.

domain_ai_inference.explanation: Streaming weights and activations through GPU memory mixes reads and writes.

domain_data_science.explanation: Transforming large arrays reads input and writes output in GPU memory.

domain_server_virt.explanation: Shared GPU servers divide copy bandwidth across many tenants.
